reasoning: The current best model is XGBoost (MAE 20.37) using the full feature set (`data/serie_features.csv`). A critical observation is the high variance in MAE across folds (e.g., 11.81 vs 27.75 for the best model), suggesting overfitting or a failure to adapt to changing series dynamics in later periods. The previous attempt to align the objective with MAE (`reg:absoluteerror`) did not yield an improvement. Therefore, the focus must shift to explicit regularization to combat overfitting and the generation of new features that capture volatility or regime changes.

propuestas.descripcion: Optimize XGBoost with explicit regularization parameters (gamma, reg_alpha, reg_lambda, min_child_weight) to combat overfitting and reduce MAE variance across folds.
propuestas.evidencia: This directly addresses the high MAE variance
